browser_validation_guidelines.overview: Essential guidance for validating Carbon Design System components in the browser, ensuring they meet visual, functional, and accessibility requirements through dedicated subtasks.

managing_image_limits.when_to_split: When testing requires more than 8-10 screenshots, create nested subtasks

strategies.strategy: By variation: default, compact, with-icon, etc.
By state: static (default, disabled), interactive (hover, focus), error states
By feature: visual rendering, functionality, accessibility

managing_image_limits.example:
<new_task> <mode>carbon-react</mode> <message>Validate [component] at mobile breakpoints (320px, 375px, 414px). Test layout and functionality.</message> </new_task>
<new_task> <mode>carbon-react</mode> <message>Validate [component] at desktop breakpoints (1280px, 1920px). Test full layout and features.</message> </new_task>

validation_workflow.title: Browser Validation Workflow
validation_workflow.description: Browser validation is a critical step in the Carbon component implementation process. It ensures that components render correctly, function as expected, and meet accessibility standards. Always use a dedicated subtask for browser validation to maintain focus and clarity. For extensive testing, split into nested subtasks as described above.

guidance.point: Use the new_task tool to create a validation subtask
Clearly describe what needs to be validated

step.example: <new_task> <mode>carbon-react</mode> <message>Validate the implementation of [component name]. Please check for visual accuracy, responsive behavior, and functionality.</message> </new_task>

guidance.point: Use execute_command to start the development server
Wait for the server to be ready

step.action: Launch the browser and navigate to the component

guidance.point: Use browser_action to launch the browser
Resize the browser to test different breakpoints
Take screenshots at different viewport sizes
Test all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, etc.)
Clean up by closing the browser

criteria.criterion: Typography uses appropriate Carbon type tokens
Component adapts appropriately to all breakpoints
Layout changes follow Carbon grid guidelines
All interactive elements function as expected

scenario.name: Form Component Validation
scenario.description: Validating a form component with input fields, validation, and submission.

scenario.name: Data Table Validation
scenario.description: Validating a data table component with sorting, pagination, and row selection.

scenario.name: Modal Dialog Validation
scenario.description: Validating a modal dialog component with opening, closing, and interaction.

key_steps.step: Test keyboard accessibility (Tab navigation and Escape to close)

section.name: Summary
section.content: Brief overview of the component and validation results. Include overall pass/fail status and major findings.

section.name: Visual Accuracy
section.content: Assessment of the component's visual appearance compared to requirements. Include screenshots and notes on any discrepancies.

section.name: Responsive Behavior
section.content: Assessment of the component's behavior across different screen sizes. Include screenshots at different breakpoints and notes on any issues.

section.name: Functionality
section.content: Assessment of the component's interactive features. Include descriptions of tested interactions and any issues found.

section.name: Accessibility
section.content: Assessment of the component's accessibility features. Include notes on keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, and any issues found.

section.name: Issues and Recommendations
section.content: List of identified issues with recommendations for fixes. Prioritize issues by severity.
